title:
@nichochar: this is a fantastic read

2026 will see tons of evolution around agent harnesses...
rawContent:
Tweet by @nichochar (Nicholas Charriere)
Author bio: ceo @get_mocha (YC S23) 

 ex @cruise @pinterest 

i like building legos that empower others to build castles
Text: this is a fantastic read

2026 will see tons of evolution around agent harnesses. Beside models, they're the highest leverage point for improvement.

I think the article could go in more depth around the communication protocol between agents. It mentions files as the handoff between the evaluator and the generator, but memory seems like a core part of recursive improvement harnesses and is under-discussed.

Some small things I found interesting:
- In the plan, they have Claude write a lot of prose over bullet points (codex prompts use a similar trick)
- ~200$ for a little app seems expensive but is much cheaper than using humans
- this is still zero-shot apps. You'd need to design a different harness for working in existing codebases (cf stuff @zeeg has been saying)
- QA and LLM as evaluators is going to be huge. It was a thing in LLM evaluation frameworks but I think it's going to become a "primitive" in the harness world.
- When models get better, the harness gets simpler (he killed "sprints" from 4.5 -> 4.6)
